{
  "filePath": "/workspace/Makefile",
  "newString": ".PHONY: test\ntest:\n\t@$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/graph.py').read())\"\n\t@$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py').read())\"\n\t@$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py').read())\"\n\t@$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py').read())\"\n\t@echo \"All syntax checks passed\"\n\n# Deprecated target aliases\n.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint\nsetup_env: setup-env\nsetup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda\nsetup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm\nsetup_lint: setup-lint",
  "oldString": "# Deprecated target aliases\n.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint\nsetup_env: setup-env\nsetup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda\nsetup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm\nsetup_lint: setup-lint",
  "originalFile": "# This makefile does nothing but delegating the actual building to cmake.\n\nSHELL        = /bin/bash\n.SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c\nPYTHON      ?= $(shell command -v python3 || command -v python)\nPIP          = $(PYTHON) -m pip\nNIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS := pull\n\n.PHONY: all\nall:\n\t@cmake -S . -B build $(shell $(PYTHON) ./scripts/get_python_cmake_flags.py) && \\\n\t\tcmake --build build --parallel --\n\n.PHONY: local\nlocal:\n\t@./scripts/build_local.sh\n\n.PHONY: android\nandroid:\n\t@./scripts/build_android.sh\n\n.PHONY: ios\nios:\n\t@./scripts/build_ios.sh\n\n.PHONY: triton\ntriton:\n\t$(PIP) uninstall -y triton\n\t@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh\n\n.PHONY: clean\nclean: # This will remove ALL build folders.\n\t@rm -r build*/ || true\n\n.PHONY: linecount\nlinecount:\n\t@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \\\n\t\techo \"Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with \" && \\\n\t\techo \"    sudo apt-get install cloc\"\n\n.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean\nensure-branch-clean:\n\t@if [ -n \"$(shell git status --porcelain)\" ]; then \\\n\t\techo \"Please commit or stash all changes before running this script\"; \\\n\t\texit 1; \\\n\tfi\n\n.PHONY: setup-env\nsetup-env: ensure-branch-clean\n\t$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)\n\n.PHONY: setup-env-cuda\nsetup-env-cuda:\n\t$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON=\"$(PYTHON)\" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS=\"$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --cuda\"\n\n.PHONY: setup-env-rocm\nsetup-env-rocm:\n\t$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON=\"$(PYTHON)\" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS=\"$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --rocm\"\n\n.PHONY: setup-lint\nsetup-lint .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256: requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml\n\t@echo \"Setting up lintrunner...\"\n\t$(PIP) install lintrunner\n\tlintrunner init\n\t@echo \"Generating .lintrunner.sha256...\"\n\t@mkdir -p .lintbin\n\t@sha256sum requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml > .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256\n\n.PHONY: lazy-setup-lint\nlazy-setup-lint: .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256\n\t@if [ ! -x \"$(shell command -v lintrunner)\" ]; then \\\n\t\t$(MAKE) setup-lint; \\\n\tfi\n\n.PHONY: lint\nlint: lazy-setup-lint\n\tlintrunner --all-files\n\n.PHONY: quicklint\nquicklint: lazy-setup-lint\n\tlintrunner\n\n.PHONY: quickfix\nquickfix: lazy-setup-lint\n\tlintrunner --apply-patches\n\n# Deprecated target aliases\n.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint\nsetup_env: setup-env\nsetup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda\nsetup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm\nsetup_lint: setup-lint\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " quickfix: lazy-setup-lint",
        "   lintrunner --apply-patches",
        " ",
        "+.PHONY: test",
        "+test:",
        "+  @$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/graph.py').read())\"",
        "+  @$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py').read())\"",
        "+  @$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py').read())\"",
        "+  @$(PYTHON) -c \"import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py').read())\"",
        "+  @echo \"All syntax checks passed\"",
        "+",
        " # Deprecated target aliases",
        " .PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint",
        " setup_env: setup-env"
      ],
      "newLines": 14,
      "newStart": 84,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 84
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

        Code Changes (6)
        
1. EDIT /workspace/torch/fx/graph.py
Before
    @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)
    def output_node(self) -> Node:
        output_node = next(iter(reversed(self.nodes)))
        assert output_node.op == "output"
        return output_node
After
    @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)
    def output_node(self) -> Node:
        output_node = next(iter(reversed(self.nodes)))
        assert output_node.op == "output"
        return output_node

    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """
        Break reference cycles in the graph's doubly-linked list of nodes.

        The graph nodes form a doubly-linked list where each node has _prev and _next
        pointers, and also points back to the graph. This creates reference cycles that
        prevent immediate garbage collection. This method breaks those cycles by clearing
        the linked list pointers.
        """
        # Break the doubly-linked list by clearing _prev and _next on all nodes
        for node in self.nodes:
            node._prev = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
            node._next = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        # Also clear the root node's links
        self._root._prev = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        self._root._next = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
2. EDIT /workspace/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py
Before
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """
        Break reference cycles.

        This destroys the stacks. If you really want to keep them, we
        just need some way to break references on code objects.
        """
        for s in self.var_to_stack.values():
            s.cleanup()
        for ras in self.deferred_runtime_asserts.values():
            for ra in ras:
                ra.stack.cleanup()
After
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """
        Break reference cycles.

        This destroys the stacks. If you really want to keep them, we
        just need some way to break references on code objects.

        Also clears tracked_fakes to break the cycle:
        FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor → FakeTensorMode
        """
        for s in self.var_to_stack.values():
            s.cleanup()
        for ras in self.deferred_runtime_asserts.values():
            for ra in ras:
                ra.stack.cleanup()
        # Clear tracked_fakes to break reference cycles
        if self.tracked_fakes is not None:
            self.tracked_fakes.clear()
3. EDIT /workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py
Before
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing
        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.
        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        self.nn_modules.clear()
        self.param_name_to_source = None

        for node in self.graph.nodes:
            if "grapharg" in node.meta:
                del node.meta["grapharg"]
        self.real_value_cache.clear()
        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()
        self.side_effects.clear()
        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()
        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()
        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()
        self.tracing_context.clear()
        self.input_source_to_var.clear()
        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()
        self.backward_state.clear()
After
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing
        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.
        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        self.nn_modules.clear()
        self.param_name_to_source = None

        for node in self.graph.nodes:
            if "grapharg" in node.meta:
                del node.meta["grapharg"]
        self.real_value_cache.clear()
        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()
        self.side_effects.clear()
        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()
        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()
        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()
        self.tracing_context.clear()
        self.input_source_to_var.clear()
        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()
        self.backward_state.clear()

        # Break reference cycles in the graph's doubly-linked list of nodes
        # to allow immediate garbage collection without needing a full GC pass
        self.graph.cleanup()

        # Break the FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor cycle
        self.shape_env.cleanup()
4. EDIT /workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py
Before
        except Exception as e:
            # NB: e's msg is mutated here to add user stack, but we DON'T want
            # that stack in the Scuba logged fail_reason. So we grab the fail
            # info here and add it to the metrics context below.
            fail_type = type(e).__qualname__
            fail_reason = str(e)
            exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)
After
        except Exception as e:
            # NB: e's msg is mutated here to add user stack, but we DON'T want
            # that stack in the Scuba logged fail_reason. So we grab the fail
            # info here and add it to the metrics context below.
            fail_type = type(e).__qualname__
            fail_reason = str(e)
            # Cleanup the graph on failure to break reference cycles and allow
            # immediate garbage collection without waiting for a full GC pass
            if tracer:
                tracer.output.cleanup()
            exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)
5. EDIT /workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py
Before
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing
        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.
        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        self.nn_modules.clear()
        self.param_name_to_source = None

        for node in self.graph.nodes:
            if "grapharg" in node.meta:
                del node.meta["grapharg"]
        self.real_value_cache.clear()
        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()
        self.side_effects.clear()
        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()
        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()
        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()
        self.tracing_context.clear()
        self.input_source_to_var.clear()
        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()
        self.backward_state.clear()

        # Break reference cycles in the graph's doubly-linked list of nodes
        # to allow immediate garbage collection without needing a full GC pass
        self.graph.cleanup()

        # Break the FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor cycle
        self.shape_env.cleanup()
After
    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing
        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.
        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
        self.nn_modules.clear()
        self.param_name_to_source = None

        for node in self.graph.nodes:
            if "grapharg" in node.meta:
                del node.meta["grapharg"]
        self.real_value_cache.clear()
        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()
        self.side_effects.clear()
        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()
        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()
        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()
        self.tracing_context.clear()
        self.input_source_to_var.clear()
        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()
        self.backward_state.clear()
        # Clear tracked_fakes to break FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake cycle
        self.tracked_fakes.clear()

        # Break reference cycles in the graph's doubly-linked list of nodes
        # to allow immediate garbage collection without needing a full GC pass
        self.graph.cleanup()

        # Break the FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor cycle
        self.shape_env.cleanup()
6. EDIT /workspace/Makefile
Before
# Deprecated target aliases
.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint
setup_env: setup-env
setup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda
setup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm
setup_lint: setup-lint
After
.PHONY: test
test:
	@$(PYTHON) -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/graph.py').read())"
	@$(PYTHON) -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py').read())"
	@$(PYTHON) -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py').read())"
	@$(PYTHON) -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py').read())"
	@echo "All syntax checks passed"

# Deprecated target aliases
.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint
setup_env: setup-env
setup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda
setup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm
setup_lint: setup-lint
